rule_text: Exploit time decay differential in calendar spreads by buying shorter-term options and selling longer-term options of the same type and strike, since near-term options decay faster than far-term options when the underlying price remains stable.
entry_conditions: Underlying price stability: no movement > 0.5% in prior month
Time difference between legs: 1-2 months (e.g., March vs May)
Both options at-the-money or near-the-money
Implied volatility rank below 40th percentile
exit_conditions: Exit when spread reaches 50% of max profit
Exit if underlying moves > 1% against direction
Exit 2 weeks before near-term expiration
haiku_reason: All entry conditions are specific and measurable (price movement threshold, time difference range, moneyness criteria, IV rank percentile) and exit conditions are clearly defined with actionable triggers (profit target percentage, price movement threshold, calendar-based exit).

rule_text: A trader should exploit arbitrage opportunities when the synthetic option position (long call + short put) diverges from the underlying forward price, as equality between call−put and underlying−strike indicates fair value parity.
entry_conditions: Calculate synthetic parity: call_price − put_price vs underlying_price − strike_price
Identify absolute deviation: |actual_synthetic − theoretical_synthetic|
Enter only when deviation ≥ 0.05 in favor of arbitrage side
exit_conditions: Synthetic returns to parity or within 0.02 of parity
Position held to expiration for mechanical convergence
haiku_reason: The signal has specific quantitative entry conditions (calculate synthetic parity, measure absolute deviation ≥0.05) and objective exit conditions (parity within 0.02) that a programmer can implement using market data for option prices, underlying price, and strike price.

rule_text: A trader should execute a conversion (long stock + short synthetic) only when the carrying cost of the locked-in debit is less than the profit margin built into the trade, accounting for the time to expiration and prevailing interest rates.
entry_conditions: Call price - Put price is sufficiently overpriced relative to stock price - exercise price
Net profit margin (credit received - debit carried) ≥ 2 × (carrying_cost_percentage × debit_amount)
Interest rate × time_to_expiration product is known and quantified
exit_conditions: Hold to expiration and deliver/receive stock at exercise price
Close if mispricing narrows before expiration
haiku_reason: The signal has specific, quantifiable entry conditions including call-put price relationships, a mathematical profit margin formula (net profit ≥ 2 × carrying cost), and interest rate calculations that a programmer could code to detect from market data.

rule_text: A trader should exercise deep in-the-money put options early rather than hold them when their market price falls significantly below parity value and remains below parity even under favorable volatility scenarios.

Put price < parity even if IV rises to current_IV + 400bps
Days to expiration <= 30
Moneyness (intrinsic value / option price) >= 1.75
exit_conditions: Execute early exercise immediately upon confirmation of entry conditions
haiku_reason: All entry conditions are quantifiable metrics (option price vs parity value, IV scenarios, days to expiration, moneyness ratio) that can be programmatically detected from market data, with a clear immediate action trigger upon confirmation.

Position gamma exposure reaches threshold

Calculate PV of all dividends before expiration using risk-free rate

rule_text: A jelly roll position should be entered when the observed price difference between the call time spread and put time spread deviates from their theoretical equilibrium relationship (differing by the roll's intrinsic value).
entry_conditions: Absolute deviation of (call_time_spread_price - put_time_spread_price) from roll_theoretical_value exceeds 2.0 points
Bid-ask spread on constituent legs is less than 0.25 points
Both spreads are actively traded with tight quoted spreads
exit_conditions: Price difference converges to within 0.25 points of theoretical value
Time decay erodes position value beyond 15% of entry capital
Either spread becomes illiquid (bid-ask > 0.5 points)
haiku_reason: All three entry conditions specify quantifiable thresholds (2.0 point deviation, 0.25 point bid-ask spread, tight quotes) that can be programmatically detected from market data, and exit conditions are similarly measurable (0.25 point convergence, 15% value erosion, 0.5 point liquidity threshold).

rule_text: A trader should exploit mispricing when the observed call-put price differential deviates from the theoretical synthetic relationship adjusted for carrying costs.
entry_conditions: Calculate fair call-put spread: (exercise_price × risk_free_rate × time_to_expiration) / 252
Measure observed spread: call_price - put_price
Entry if |observed_spread - fair_spread| > 1.5 × fair_spread
exit_conditions: Exit when observed spread converges within 0.3× fair_spread of theoretical value
Exit at expiration or when time decay dominates
haiku_reason: The signal specifies quantifiable entry conditions (calculating fair spreads, measuring observed spreads, and comparing deviations with a 1.5x threshold) that can be programmatically detected from market data, along with defined exit triggers based on convergence thresholds.

rule_text: A trader should construct spread positions by analyzing the delta relationship between contract legs to establish directional bias, then size the position to match the desired net delta exposure across both contracts.
entry_conditions: Two correlated futures contracts identified with opposite delta bias
Desired net delta exposure calculated as ≤25% of total absolute delta sum
exit_conditions: Net delta drifts >0.10 from target due to price movement
Spread relationship breaks correlation threshold
haiku_reason: The signal contains specific, actionable entry conditions (identifying correlated contracts with opposite delta bias and calculating net delta exposure ≤25% of absolute delta sum) and exit conditions (net delta drift >0.10 from target, correlation threshold breach) that can be programmatically detected from market data and portfolio state.

rule_text: A trader should construct a calendar spread by buying a longer-dated option and selling a shorter-dated option at the same strike, profiting from differential theta decay when the underlying is stable.
entry_conditions: Underlying within ±1% of entry level
Time to short option expiration: 4-6 weeks
Time to long option expiration: 6+ months
IV rank < 50th percentile (favor lower volatility environments)
exit_conditions: Underlying moves beyond ±2% of entry
Short leg reaches 21 DTE with >50% max profit
Time decay reversal (long leg theta becomes negative relative to short)
haiku_reason: The signal has specific, quantifiable entry conditions (underlying within ±1%, DTE ranges, IV rank < 50th percentile) and exit conditions (price moves beyond ±2%, specific DTE and profit thresholds) that a programmer could detect from market data.

rule_text: A trader should evaluate synthetic equivalence relationships across related strikes and instruments before executing a directional options strategy, as a more favorable price in a synthetic substitute may offer better risk-adjusted execution than the original intended trade.
entry_conditions: Intended call or put strategy is quoted at price P1
Synthetic equivalent is available at price P2
Synthetic price advantage >= 2.0% of strike price
Both instruments have identical expiration and underlying
exit_conditions: Price advantage closes to < 1.0%
Wider bid-ask spread in synthetic instrument develops
Execute when synthetic advantage confirmed across 2+ price checks
haiku_reason: The signal has specific, measurable entry conditions (price P1 vs P2 with >=2.0% threshold, identical expiration/underlying) and exit conditions (spread widening, percentage thresholds) that can be programmatically detected from market data.

Do not initiate long calls under these conditions

rule_text: A trader should purchase a protective option (call or put) to cap maximum loss on an underlying position when facing adverse price movement risk in one direction.
entry_conditions: Position exists in underlying asset (long or short)
Protective option strike is at or within 1% of current underlying price
Position sizing equals 100% of underlying exposure
exit_conditions: Underlying position closed
Option expires worthless and position still open (re-hedge decision required)
Loss cap breached (exercise protective option)
haiku_reason: The entry conditions specify measurable portfolio states (position exists, strike price within 1% of current price, position sizing equals 100%) that can be detected programmatically from market data and portfolio records.

rule_text: A trader should exploit the mean-reversion tendency of implied volatility relative to historical volatility by taking positions that profit when implied volatility converges toward historical volatility levels, recognizing that IV typically under-responds to HV changes.
entry_conditions: Historical volatility increases by >= 10 points
Implied volatility increases by < 5 points (less than 50% of HV move)
Divergence sustained for >= 3 bars
exit_conditions: IV-HV ratio converges to 0.75+
Losses exceed 2% of account
Time decay reduces position to insignificance

rule_text: A trader should monitor interest rate levels and adjust long-term in-the-money American put positions defensively, as rising interest rates increase early exercise probability by improving the opportunity cost of holding the option versus exercising it.

It is common for traders to take bull or bear market positions by choosing appropriate

rule_text: A trader prioritizing directional conviction should select spreads where delta stability (directional characteristics preservation) is the primary concern and volatility sensitivity is secondary, ensuring the spread maintains its intended directional bias across market moves.

Vega exposure of candidate spread is minimal (vega < 10% of delta notional)

somewhere within
rule_text: A trader should price vertical spreads by estimating the probability distribution of the underlying's final position relative to the strike prices, then expect the spread price to reflect the likelihood of expiration outcomes.

Time decay accelerates (last 7 days to expiration)

rule_text: When implied volatility is believed to be too high, avoid spreads with at-the-money options because they suffer the greatest total point overpricing; instead, select spreads constructed away from the money to minimize vega exposure to overpriced volatility.

Implied volatility > 2.0 standard deviations above 60-day mean
Construct spread with both strikes equidistant from ATM, minimum 10 delta away from money

rule_text: A trader should adjust spread delta expectations and hedge positioning based on volatility forecast accuracy, because spread deltas converge toward 50 when realized volatility exceeds estimates and diverge away from 50 when realized volatility falls short of estimates.

Realized volatility calculated over rolling 5-day window
Absolute difference between realized and implied volatility ≥ 3%

rule_text: A trader should construct a zero-cost or positive-expected-value spread by identifying and exploiting relative mispricing between two related spreads, purchasing the underpriced spread and selling the overpriced spread regardless of directional bias, when both spreads have identical market prices but differ in intrinsic theoretical value.
entry_conditions: Bid-ask midpoint prices of two spreads are identical or within 0.05 cents
Theoretical value difference between spreads exceeds 0.50 cents (5 bps of spread width)
Interest rate and dividend assumptions have not changed >5 bps in past 5 trading days
Implied volatility term structure is monotonic or flat (no inversion)
exit_conditions: Position achieves target profit of 0.25 cents or more
Bid-ask spread widens beyond 0.20 cents
Liquidity dries up and cannot unwind within 1 minute
haiku_reason: All four entry conditions are specific and quantifiable (midpoint prices within 0.05 cents, theoretical value difference >0.50 cents, rate/dividend changes <5 bps, IV term structure monotonicity), and a programmer could implement automated detection logic using market data and pricing models.

rule_text: When an American option has delta near 100 and theoretical value equals intrinsic value, a trader should compare the cost-benefit of three equivalent delta positions (hold option, exercise option, or replicate with futures) and select the choice with lowest transaction costs and highest capital efficiency.

rule_text: A trader holding an American option should actively reposition (exercise, sell and buy stock substitute, or exit entirely) before a dividend ex-date rather than passively hold, because holding exposes the position to certain loss equal to the dividend amount due to parity collapse in the option price.

Known dividend ex-date within 5 trading days

rule_text: A trader should execute a short stock + short deep ITM put spread to capture risk-free interest on the combined exercise/sale proceeds, holding until put assignment or expiration.

rule_text: Sell index options when implied volatility is persistently higher than historical volatility, as this indicates systematic overpricing relative to theoretical fair value.
entry_conditions: Implied volatility > Historical volatility by at least 15%
IV-to-HV spread maintained for at least 5 consecutive trading days
Absolute IV level above 20th percentile of 1-year range
exit_conditions: IV-to-HV spread compresses below 5%
Realized loss exceeds 2% of position notional
Calendar exit at 15 days to expiration
haiku_reason: All entry and exit conditions are quantifiable (IV vs HV spreads, percentile thresholds, time-based triggers) and can be programmatically detected from market data.

rule_text: When constructing multi-leg spreads with instruments of unequal point values, a trader must adjust position sizes to equalize dollar exposure across legs, not just notional points, to prevent unintended directional bias and losses from differential performance.
entry_conditions: Dollar value of long leg equals dollar value of short leg (within ±1%)
Point value ratios calculated and verified before position entry
Position size ratio = (Point_Value_Short / Point_Value_Long) × (Quantity_Target)
exit_conditions: Exit entire spread if dollar exposure drifts beyond ±2% due to price movement
Rebalance legs if tracking error exceeds tolerance
haiku_reason: The signal contains specific, quantifiable entry conditions (dollar value equality within ±1%, point value ratio verification, position size formula) and exit conditions (±2% drift threshold, tracking error tolerance) that can be programmatically detected from market data and portfolio state.

rule_text: A trader should establish a delta-neutral hedge between two correlated underlying instruments when comparing option theoretical value to actual price, so that profits come from relative strength differences rather than directional moves.
entry_conditions: ABC option mispricing > 2% vs theoretical value
Delta offset between ABC and XYZ positions = 99-101% (near-perfect neutrality)
Correlation between ABC and XYZ > 0.85
Both instruments have sufficient liquidity for hedging
exit_conditions: Delta drift exceeds ±2% of position notional
Mispricing converges within 0.5% of theoretical
Correlation falls below 0.80
haiku_reason: All entry and exit conditions specify quantifiable thresholds (mispricing %, delta offset %, correlation levels, liquidity metrics) that can be programmatically detected from market data and portfolio state.

rule_text: When a trader has a directional volatility opinion on a correlated multi-leg spread, substitute options for futures in the legs where volatility is mispriced, rather than using futures across all legs.

Implied volatility in target leg deviates from 20-day historical volatility by ≥1.5 standard deviations
Correlation between spread legs is ≥0.75
Futures baseline spread (e.g., 3:2:1 crack spread) is at acceptable profit level
exit_conditions: Implied vol converges to historical vol (deviation <0.5 SD)
Correlation between legs breaks below 0.65

rule_text: A trader with only a volatility opinion should construct intermarket spreads by simultaneously buying options in one market and selling options in the other market, sized to exploit perceived mispricing in the volatility relationship between two correlated assets.
entry_conditions: Identify two correlated markets (historical correlation >= 0.70)

Confirm mispricing persists across 2+ consecutive periods
exit_conditions: IV spread reverts to within 0.5 standard deviations of mean
Correlation drops below 0.60
Absolute loss reaches 2% of notional spread value

rule_text: A trader should establish a short volatility spread (short straddle structure) when Greeks indicate negative gamma and vega with positive theta, signaling a belief that implied volatilities across markets are too high and will decline or mean-revert.
entry_conditions: Gamma < -500 (strong negative convexity)
Vega < -1200 (significant negative vega exposure)
Theta > +100 (substantial time decay benefit)
Implied volatility percentile > 80th (very elevated)
IV rank or IV percentile confirms top quartile volatility environment
exit_conditions: IV falls below 50th percentile OR
Underlying moves >2 standard deviations AND position loss exceeds 15% of spread debit/credit OR
Theta decay target met (50% of max theta captured)
haiku_reason: All entry conditions are quantifiable (gamma, vega, theta thresholds, IV percentile ranks) that can be programmatically detected from options market data, and exit conditions are specific numeric triggers or calculated metrics.

rule_text: In a ratio call spread, actively manage delta exposure by monitoring implied volatility changes across price levels, since falling volatility makes the position delta-positive and rising volatility makes it delta-negative.
entry_conditions: Establish ratio call spread (long OTM calls, short more ATM calls)
Record implied volatility at entry (baseline IV)
Set delta monitoring threshold at ±5 delta points from neutral (45-55 range)
exit_conditions: Exit or hedge when delta exceeds ±5 delta points from 50
Exit when IV falls more than 2% below entry IV (excessive positive delta)
Exit when IV rises more than 2% above entry IV (excessive negative delta)
haiku_reason: The signal contains specific, actionable entry conditions (establish ratio call spread, record IV baseline, set delta threshold at ±5 points) and quantifiable exit triggers (delta exceeds ±5 from 50, IV changes >2%) that can be detected programmatically from market data and position metrics.

rule_text: A trader should monitor and rebalance delta exposure when volatility changes, because rising volatility extends delta towards 50 (creating long exposure) while falling volatility pushes delta away from 50 (creating short exposure), both of which distort the intended position structure.

Volatility level recorded at entry
Calculate target delta range: ±8 ticks from entry delta
exit_conditions: Execute rebalance trade when delta moves beyond ±8 from entry
Re-measure and reset tolerance bands after rebalance
haiku_reason: The signal has specific, actionable entry conditions (position delta profile, volatility level, ±8 tick tolerance bands) and exit conditions (delta movement beyond tolerance) that a programmer could implement with market data and portfolio state.

rule_text: A trader holding a short volatility position with significant negative theta should reduce theta risk exposure when the underlying moves slowly over an extended period, as daily decay accelerates losses.
entry_conditions: Position has negative theta (short volatility or short premium)
Daily price range < 0.5% of stock price for 3 consecutive days
Cumulative theta decay > 50% of initial position profit buffer
exit_conditions: Close or hedge entire short volatility position
Buy back short premium or exit spread
haiku_reason: All three entry conditions are specific and quantifiable: negative theta can be measured from position data, daily price range < 0.5% is calculable from OHLC data, and cumulative theta decay versus profit buffer can be tracked from option pricing models.

rule_text: A trader should compare their presumed delta (based on own volatility assumptions) against implied delta (market-derived) and adjust hedging decisions when the divergence is material, acknowledging that volatility assumption uncertainty makes delta calculations inherently unreliable.

Calculate implied delta from market option prices

If divergence > 5 delta points, rehedge using implied delta
exit_conditions: When presumed and implied delta converge within 5 points
When market reprices volatility toward your assumption
haiku_reason: The signal has specific, quantifiable entry conditions (calculate presumed delta, calculate implied delta, measure divergence > 5 points) and exit conditions that a programmer could implement with market data and portfolio calculations.

rule_text: The timing of volatility occurrence matters significantly for options, especially at-the-money options with high gamma; volatility realized near expiration has greater impact than the same volatility realized earlier in the option's life.
entry_conditions: Option is at-the-money (strike within 1% of underlying)
Days to expiration > 15% of original contract life
Implied volatility is below 60th percentile of historical range
exit_conditions: Days to expiration falls below 15% of original life
Implied volatility exceeds 60th percentile
Option moves out of ATM band
haiku_reason: All entry and exit conditions are specific, measurable, and can be programmed using standard market data (strike price, underlying price, days to expiration, implied volatility percentiles).

Short straddle position identified as candidate
exit_conditions: Exit when days_to_expiration < 30

rule_text: Volatility is directionally dependent—rising markets in certain asset classes (agricultural, precious metals) exhibit higher volatility than falling markets, while falling markets in others (equities, bonds) exhibit higher volatility than rising markets. rule_text: A trader should sell options (calls or puts) when the underlying price movement is expected to stay outside the strike price, allowing full premium capture at expiration.
entry_conditions: Underlying price is significantly distant from strike price
Expected move at expiration leaves large buffer zone
Sell call when underlying + (buffer margin × ATR) < strike
Sell put when underlying - (buffer margin × ATR) > strike

exit_conditions: Close position if underlying approaches strike price minus 50% of buffer
Exit at 75% of max profit (premium collected × 0.75)
Mandatory exit 7 days before expiration
haiku_reason: The signal contains specific, mathematically quantifiable entry conditions (price distance from strike, ATR-based buffer calculations) and explicit exit rules (75% max profit, 7-day expiration threshold) that a programmer could implement in code.

Expected move (based on historical or forecast) > 1.5x the ATM straddle breakeven width

rule_text: A trader should prioritize developing skill in predicting volatility/speed of the underlying market before entering options trading, because options strategies can profit from speed alone independent of direction, whereas directional prediction alone is insufficient for options success.
entry_conditions: Historical volatility prediction accuracy >= 70% over last 20 trades
Realized volatility within predicted range on >= 75% of recent trades
Trader has explicitly documented volatility edge vs market-implied volatility
exit_conditions: Accuracy falls below 65% over rolling 20-trade window
Switch to directional underlying trading if accuracy cannot be maintained
haiku_reason: The entry conditions specify quantifiable thresholds (70% accuracy, 75% success rate, documented edge) that can be programmatically verified from trade history and volatility data, and exit conditions define clear numerical triggers (65% accuracy threshold) for rule-based detection.

rule_text: A trader should only enter a stock position when the expected return (including dividends received) exceeds or equals the carrying costs over the holding period, otherwise the position will not break even.
entry_conditions: Calculate total carrying costs (interest, fees, repo costs) over defined holding period
Sum all expected dividends during holding period
Expected stock appreciation + dividends >= carrying costs × 1.25 (25% safety margin)
exit_conditions: Exit if carrying costs accumulate beyond break-even threshold before expected returns materialize
Exit at end of holding period or when cumulative costs exceed initial safety margin
haiku_reason: The rule contains specific, quantifiable entry conditions (calculate carrying costs, sum dividends, compare expected return + dividends to 1.25× carrying costs) that a programmer could implement using market data and portfolio metrics.

Volatility calculation is primary focus
No explicit interest rate adjustment needed intra-model
exit_conditions: Position expires or is closed

rule_text: A trader should construct vertical spreads anchored at-the-money to stabilize delta behavior across volatility regimes, and should prefer in-the-money spread legs over out-of-the-money legs when implied volatility assessment is uncertain.

Second leg is in-the-money option (moneyness offset ≤ 0.5 delta from ATM)

Exit when realized volatility clearly confirms IV estimate direction

rule_text: A trader should select a bull vertical spread (ITM call spread) when market direction prediction favors continuation or stability, and a bear spread (OTM call spread) when prediction is uncertain or directionally bearish, because ITM spreads benefit from time decay regardless of directional movement while OTM spreads require directional movement to profit.

rule_text: A trader should exploit synthetic-real price discrepancies by selling the overpriced instrument pair and buying the underpriced equivalent, using the three-way parity relationship between calls, puts, and underlying contracts.

Exit on maximum hold period of 30 days before expiration

rule_text: A trader should exercise an American option early when the present value of immediate cash flow from exercise exceeds the time value remaining in the option.

exit_conditions: Exercise immediately upon condition met
Do not hold for potential further appreciation
haiku_reason: The rule has specific, quantifiable entry conditions that can be programmed: calculate intrinsic value plus dividends, estimate time value using established models, and trigger when immediate cash flow exceeds 1.5x the remaining time value.

rule_text: A trader should ensure sufficient capital reserves to maintain synthetic positions through adverse price moves, because forced liquidation due to margin/funding constraints will always result in losses regardless of the underlying position's theoretical profitability.

Exit if funding requirement grows beyond available capital

rule_text: A trader should construct box spreads by simultaneously executing both legs (call vertical and put vertical) when both can be traded at prices that collectively offer arbitrage value relative to the box's theoretical value.

rule_text: A trader should maintain sufficient liquid capital reserves to cover the possibility of early assignment on short options positions, treating it as a structural risk equivalent to price movement or volatility changes.

Assignment occurs and cash settled

rule_text: A trader holding a voluntary long position should hedge downside risk using options or futures when expecting short-term decline, choosing the hedge method based on its cost relative to the cost of liquidating and re-entering the position.

Decline occurs and hedge profit offsets position loss
Risk environment improves (decline probability falls below 25%)

rule_text: A trader should balance long and short call positions to control upside risk exposure, and balance long and short put positions to control downside risk exposure, because unequal positions create unlimited directional risk.
entry_conditions: Count long calls in position
Count short calls in position
Count long puts in position
Count short puts in position
Verify calls are perfectly balanced (long calls = short calls)
Verify puts are perfectly balanced (long puts = short puts)
exit_conditions: Exit immediately if long calls ≠ short calls
Exit immediately if long puts ≠ short puts
haiku_reason: The signal has specific, actionable entry conditions (counting and comparing long/short call and put positions) that a programmer can directly implement by querying portfolio state data.

rule_text: A trader should calibrate their pricing model to match marketplace consensus assumptions rather than using complex theoretical models disconnected from market reality, treating the market's implicit model as the ground truth.

New market regime detected requiring re-calibration

rule_text: A trader should execute a conversion or reverse conversion when implied volatilities for calls and puts at the same strike diverge, as this signals a pricing arbitrage opportunity.
entry_conditions: IV(call, strike K) != IV(put, strike K)
Absolute difference in IV >= 2.0 percentage points
Bid-ask spread on conversion leg < 0.5% of spread width
exit_conditions: IV spread converges to <= 0.5 percentage points
Position held to expiration or delivery

rule_text: Execute conversion or reverse conversion spreads when calls and puts exhibit relative mispricings, capturing the arbitrage opportunity between the two legs.

rule_text: A trader should exploit relative mispricing within a volatility surface by selling overpriced options and buying underpriced neighbors, using the relative comparison as an edge when absolute pricing is uncertain.

Execute only when 3+ adjacent strikes show consistent directional mispricing pattern

Time decay reaches 50% of option's remaining theta
Loss exceeds 15% of spread debit/credit

rule_text: A trader should exploit discontinuities in the volatility skew by executing directional spread trades in the direction opposite to the skew anomaly, using the assumption that implied volatilities should progress smoothly across strike prices.
entry_conditions: Identify strike price where IV deviates from linear interpolation between adjacent strikes
Confirm deviation magnitude exceeds 2.0 vega points or 2.0% of local IV level
Verify skew direction is consistent with directional bias (sell high IV, buy low IV strikes)
Construct butterfly or call spread anchored at anomalous strike

Exit if position moves 50% toward maximum profit
Stop loss if anomaly widens beyond entry deviation by 1.0 vega point

rule_text: A trader should adjust the volatility skew by shifting its position and shape in response to changes in the underlying price and shifts in implied volatility opinion, using the skew's relative value structure to reflect both directional views and volatility level convictions.
entry_conditions: Underlying contract moves 2.0+ points from reference level
Implied volatility opinion shifts by ≥0.50 volatility points from market level
exit_conditions: Underlying reverts to within 1.0 point of reference level
Skew returns to market consensus
haiku_reason: Entry conditions specify quantifiable price movement thresholds (2.0+ points, ±0.50 volatility points) and exit conditions define measurable reversion levels (1.0 point, skew consensus) that a programmer could detect from market data.

rule_text: A market maker should adjust the volatility skew (shift levels or raise wings) in response to directional inventory imbalances to manage risk exposure across the strike spectrum.
entry_conditions: Net delta exposure exceeds 3 standard deviations of typical position size
Short OTM calls or puts represent >40% of total option position notional
exit_conditions: Net delta exposure returns within 1.5 standard deviations
Inventory rebalanced to within normal range
haiku_reason: Both entry conditions are quantifiable (delta exposure in standard deviations, notional percentage thresholds) and can be programmatically detected from portfolio data, though the exit action itself (how to adjust skew) is qualitative.

rule_text: A trader should compare the intrinsic exercise value of an American option against its theoretical cash-settled value adjusted for carrying costs; if intrinsic value exceeds the discounted theoretical value, early exercise is warranted and the option should be valued at intrinsic rather than time-adjusted theoretical value.
entry_conditions: Option is American (not European)
Intrinsic_value > (Theoretical_value - carrying_costs * 0.75)
Remaining_time_periods >= 1
exit_conditions: Exercise the option immediately when condition is met
Hold if margin shrinks below threshold
haiku_reason: The entry conditions contain specific quantifiable comparisons (intrinsic value vs. discounted theoretical value with carrying cost adjustment) and time period thresholds that can be programmatically evaluated from option market data.

rule_text: A trader should exercise an American option early when its intrinsic value exceeds its theoretical option value, as the option has become underpriced relative to immediate exercise.
entry_conditions: For American Call: C(i,j) < 0.95 × (U(i,j) - E)
For American Put: P(i,j) < 0.95 × (E - U(i,j))

Hold if time value premium remains >5% of intrinsic value
